1: hôy bānîm sôrĕrîm = "Woe, rebellious children (sôrĕrîm)" — sārar (H5637) = to be stubborn, rebellious, the root of the "stubborn and rebellious son" of Deut 21:18-21. neʾum YHWH laʿăśôt ʿēṣâ wĕlōʾ minnî = "declares YHWH, to carry out a plan but not from me" — the sin: independent policy-making that bypasses prophetic consultation of YHWH. wĕlinsōk massēkâ wĕlōʾ rûḥî = "and to make an alliance (massēkâ, lit. a libation/poured treaty) but not of my Spirit." The covenant negotiation with Egypt bypasses YHWH's Spirit who speaks through prophets.
2: hallĕkîm lāredet miṣrayim = "who set out to go down to Egypt" — yārad (to go down) is the standard direction for Egypt in Hebrew geography; "going down" to Egypt reverses the Exodus's "coming up." ûpî lōʾ šāʾālû = "and who did not ask my mouth (peh)" — the prophetic oracle was the legitimate means of determining YHWH's will; bypassing it means bypassing YHWH himself. laʿōz bĕmaʿôz parʿōh ûlaḥăsôt bĕṣel miṣrayim = "to seek refuge in Pharaoh's protection and shelter in Egypt's shadow" — ṣel (shadow) is an ironic image: Egypt's "shadow" provides no real shade, just an illusion of protection.
3: wĕhāyâ lākem maʿôz parʿōh lĕḇōšet = "and Pharaoh's protection (maʿôz) will become for you shame (bōšet)." The very thing sought for security becomes the occasion of disgrace. wĕhaḥăsûtyâ bĕṣel miṣrayim liklimmâ = "and the shelter (ḥāsâ) of Egypt's shadow will become humiliation (kĕlimmâ)." Both security words — maʿôz (stronghold) and ḥāsâ (shelter) — reverse to their opposites: shame and humiliation.
4: kî-hāyû bĕZôʿan śārāyw = "For his officials are at Zoan (Ṣoʿan)" — Tanis, a major Egyptian delta city, the meeting place of the Egyptian court. wĕmalʾākāyw ḥānēs yagîʿû = "and his envoys reach Hanes" — another Egyptian city (possibly Heracleopolis); the diplomatic mission has traveled deep into Egypt. The verse describes the actual journey the Judean envoys have taken — Isaiah is not speaking abstractly but about a real diplomatic mission underway.
5: kōl hikĕlîm ʿal-ʿam lōʾ yôʿîlû lāhem = "Everyone will be put to shame because of a people that cannot profit them (yāʿal)" — Egypt will not benefit Judah at all. lōʾ lĕʿezrâ wĕlōʾ lĕhôʿîl kî lĕḇōšet wĕgam lĕḥerpâ = "not for help and not for profit, but for shame and also for disgrace (ḥerpâ)." The oracle is categorical: no benefit whatsoever, only dishonor. ḥerpâ = the shame that comes from a failed partnership, worse than having sought no help at all.
6: maśśāʾ bahămôt negeḇ = "The oracle (maśśāʾ) concerning the beasts of the Negeb" — the oracle describes the caravan route through the Negeb wilderness to Egypt. bĕʾereṣ ṣārâ wĕṣûqâ lābiʾ ûlayiš mēhem ʾepʿeh ûśārāp mĕʿôpēp = "through the land of trouble and anguish, of lioness and lion, of viper and flying serpent (śārāp mĕʿôpēp)" — the wilderness through which the treasure-laden caravan travels is dangerous (cf. Isa 6:2 śārāp = the burning serpent-beings). yiśśĕʾû ʿal-ketep ʿayirim ḥêlām = "they carry their wealth on donkeys' backs" — the gifts for Egypt, the bribe for military protection. Going to enormous effort and expense for an alliance that will be worthless (v5).
7: wĕmiṣrayim hebel wĕrîq yaʿzōrû = "Egypt's help is worthless (hebel) and empty (rîq)" — hebel is Qohelet's word for vanity/vapor; here applied to Egyptian military reliability. lākēn qārāʾtî lāzōʾt Rahab hēm šāḇet = "therefore I have called this: Rahab who sits still." Rahab (H7294) = the chaos-sea-monster of OT mythology (cf. Job 9:13; 26:12; Ps 87:4; 89:10), also used as a symbolic name for Egypt. "Rahab who sits still" = the chaos-beast that does nothing — a powerless monster, all reputation and no action. The ironic title deflates Egypt's pretension.
8: ʿattâ bôʾ kĕtāḇāh ʿal-lûaḥ ʾittām wĕʿal-sēper ḥuqqĕhā = "Now go, write it before them on a tablet (lûaḥ) and inscribe it in a scroll (sēper)" — the act of writing preserves the prophecy for future vindication. wĕtihî lĕyôm ʾaḥărôn lĕʿad ʿad-ʿôlām = "that it may be for a later day a witness forever" — the prophecy is sealed into permanence (cf. 8:16). When Egypt proves useless and the Assyrian crisis comes, the written testimony will vindicate the prophet. The physical act of writing is the prophetic claim to predictive revelation.
9: kî ʿam mĕrî hû bānîm kĕḥāšîm = "For they are a rebellious people (mĕrî), lying children (kĕḥāšîm)" — mĕrî = rebellion (the noun from the same root as Miriam and Meribah); kĕḥāšîm = those who deal falsely (kāḥaš = to lie/deny). bānîm lōʾ-ʾāḇû šĕmôaʿ tôrat YHWH = "children who were not willing to hear (šāmaʿ) the tôrâ of YHWH." The diagnosis: the Egyptian alliance is not merely a political miscalculation but a symptom of refusing prophetic instruction.
10: ʾăšer ʾāmĕrû lārōʾîm lōʾ tirʾû = "who say to the seers (rōʾîm): Do not see!" — the demand to suppress prophetic vision. wĕlannĕḇîʾîm lōʾ tihzû-lānû nĕkōḥôt = "and to the prophets: Do not prophesy to us what is right (nĕkōḥôt)!" daḇĕrû-lānû ḥălāqôt = "speak to us smooth things (ḥălāqôt)" — flattering, comfortable words rather than true words. ḥězû mĕhaTtāʾôt = "prophesy illusions (mĕhaTtāʾôt)" — the demand for comfortable falsehood. 2 Tim 4:3 echoes this: "the time is coming when people will not endure sound teaching, but having itching ears they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own passions."
11: sûrû min-hadderek haTTû min-haʾōraḥ = "turn aside from the way, leave the path (ʾōraḥ)" — the demand to abandon the prophetic way entirely. haššibû mippānênû ʾet qĕdôš yiśrāʾēl = "remove from our presence the Holy One of Israel" — the most extreme demand: banish YHWH's presence from prophetic encounter. The "Holy One of Israel" is Isaiah's signature divine title (from the throne vision of ch6); to reject the prophet's message is to reject YHWH's holiness.
12: lākēn kōh-ʾāmar qĕdôš yiśrāʾēl = "Therefore thus says the Holy One of Israel" — the rejection of the title (v11) is answered by YHWH speaking with that very title. yaʿan miʾāsĕkem baḏḏāḇār hazzeh = "because you have despised (māʾas) this word" — the word māʾas (to despise, reject) is the key accusation; rejecting the prophetic word = rejecting YHWH. wattibṭĕḥû bĕʿōšeq ûlĕzût = "and you have trusted in oppression and deceit (lĕzût)" — the Egyptian alliance is characterized not just as politically unwise but as morally corrupt: built on oppression and crookedness.
13: lākēn yihyeh lākem heʿāwōn hazzeh kĕpereṣ nōpēl = "Therefore this iniquity will be to you like a bulging wall, broken and falling" — the metaphor of structural failure: pereṣ nōpēl = a breach/crack that leads to collapse. nibzĕqet bĕḥômat gāḇohâ = "a bulge in a high wall" — the crack in a high wall that gives suddenly without warning. ʾăšer pitʾōm lĕpetaʿ yāḇôʾ šibbĕrāh = "whose shattering (šāḇar) comes suddenly, in an instant (petaʿ)" — no warning, no gradual weakening visible; the collapse is sudden. The iniquity that appeared stable (the Egyptian alliance) will shatter catastrophically.
14: ûšĕḇārāh kĕšeḇer nĕḇel yôṣĕrîm kātût = "and its breaking (šiḇrāh) will be like the breaking of a potter's vessel (kĕlî yôṣĕr) smashed ruthlessly (kātût)" — the metaphor shifts from structural (wall) to ceramic (pot): irreparable destruction. wĕlōʾ-yimmāṣēʾ ḇĕmitṣāṭāyw ḥereś = "and among its fragments (ḥereś) not a shard is found" — the breaking is so complete that not even a piece large enough to carry coals or scoop water remains. Total uselessness: the alliance that was sought for survival becomes an unredeemable wreck.
15: kî-kōh ʾāmar ʾădōnāy YHWH qĕdôš yiśrāʾēl bĕšûḇâ wānāḥat tiwwāšēʿûn = "For thus says the Lord YHWH, the Holy One of Israel: In returning (šûḇâ) and rest (naḥat) you will be saved." Šûḇâ = repentance/return; naḥat = rest, settling/quietness. The paired terms are the spiritual equivalent of the military-political alliance they sought: instead of Egyptian cavalry, trust in YHWH's quiet provision. bĕhašqēṭ ûḇeḇiṭḥâ tihyeh gĕḇûratkĕm = "in quietness (hašqēṭ) and in trust (biṭḥâ) is your strength (gĕḇûrâ)" — the counter-intuitive logic of prophetic faith: the military strength (gĕḇûrâ) they sought through Egyptian cavalry is available through divine trust. wĕlōʾ ʾăḇîtem = "but you were not willing (ʾābâ)" — the volitional refusal.
16: wattōʾmĕrû lōʾ kî-ʿal-sûs nānûs = "and you said: No! for we will flee on horses" — the ironic response: they seek military mobility (horses = cavalry, speed = strategic advantage) rather than divine rest. ʿal-kēn tānûsûn = "therefore you will flee" — the wordplay: they will flee (nûs) on horses from the enemy, not toward victory. wĕʿal-qal nirkaḇ = "and we will ride swift (qal) horses" — qal = swift, light (the word of Gen 1:2 for "over the face of the waters"). ʿal-kēn yiqallû rōdĕpêkem = "therefore your pursuers will be swift" — the speed they sought in horses will be outdone by the speed of enemies pursuing them.
17: ʾelep miPPĕnê gaʿărat ʾeḥād = "a thousand before the rebuke (gaʿărâ) of one" — one enemy warrior's battle-cry will rout a thousand Judeans. miPPĕnê gaʿărat ḥămišşâ tānûsû = "before the rebuke of five, all of you will flee" — the expected ratio reverses: instead of one Israelite chasing a thousand (Deut 32:30; Lev 26:8), the equation flips. ʿad ʾim-nôtartem kĕtōren ʿal-rōʾš hāhār = "until you are left like a flagstaff (tōren) on a mountaintop" — the isolated, single flagpole on a hilltop: alone, exposed, conspicuous. The remnant reduced to a solitary marker.
18: wĕlākēn yaḥakkeh YHWH laḥankĕkem wĕlākēn yārûm lĕraḥămĕkem = "Therefore YHWH waits (ḥāḵâ) to be gracious to you (ḥānan), and therefore he exalts himself to show you mercy (raḥam)." The "therefore" is unexpected after the judgment oracles of vv15-17 — but this is YHWH's character even in judgment: he waits, ready to be gracious. kî ʾĕlōhê mišpāṭ YHWH ʾašrê kol-ḥôkê lô = "for YHWH is a God of justice (mišpāṭ); blessed (ʾašrê) are all who wait for him." The Beatitude-form (ʾašrê = blessed, the OT equivalent of NT makários) applied to those who wait for YHWH — the spiritual posture of 8:17.
19: kî-ʿam bĕṣiyyôn yēšēḇ bîrûšālāim = "for the people in Zion who dwell in Jerusalem" — the address shifts to a restored community. bākô lōʾ-tiḇkeh ḥānnôn yāḥannekā lĕqôl zaʿăqekā = "you will weep no more; he will surely be gracious to you at the sound of your cry (zaʿăqā)." Ḥānan (to be gracious) appears twice in one verse in the emphatic infinitive absolute construction (ḥānnôn yāḥannekā). The certainty of divine grace is reinforced by the grammar: "he will be-gracious-indeed gracious to you." kĕšomĕʿô ʾēleykā ʿānākā = "as soon as he hears it, he will answer you."
20: wĕnātan lākem ʾădōnāy lehem ṣar ûmayim lāḥaṣ = "And the Lord will give you the bread of adversity (ṣar) and the water of affliction (lāḥaṣ)" — even in the coming hardship, YHWH provides. wĕlōʾ-yikkānēp ʿôd môrêkā = "and your Teacher (môreh) will no longer hide himself (kānaṗ)" — the Teacher who was hidden (cf. 8:17: hiding his face) will be revealed. wĕhāyû ʿênêkā rōʾôt ʾet-môrêkā = "and your eyes will see your Teacher." The restoration of prophetic vision: the sealed scroll (29:11) reopened, the Teacher visible.
21: wĕʾoznêkā tišmaʿnâ dāḇār mēʾaḥărêkā lēʾmōr = "and your ears will hear a word from behind you saying" — the prophetic voice returning from behind, like a guide directing the traveler. zeh hadderek lĕkû-ḇô = "this is the way, walk in it." The restoration of prophetic direction: the people who demanded "speak smooth things" (30:10) will finally receive and follow true guidance. kî taʾămînû wĕkî taśmĕʾîlû = "when you turn to the right or when you turn to the left" — comprehensive direction for life, not just crisis moments.
22: wĕṭimmēʾtem ʾet-ṣippûy pĕsîlê kaspekā = "and you will defile (ṭāmēʾ) the silver overlay (ṣippûy) of your graven images." Ṭāmēʾ (to declare unclean) applied by choice to idols: the restored community will voluntarily defile their own idols — declaring them as unclean as a corpse. wĕʾet-ʾăpuddâ massēkāt zāhābekā = "and the gold plating of your cast images" — the costly metal idols are defiled. tizrēm kĕmô dāwâ ṣēʾ tōmar lô = "you will scatter them like a menstrual cloth (dāwâ), saying to them: Out!" — the most powerful image of revulsion: the idols reduced to a used sanitary rag. The reformed covenant people's total rejection of idolatry.
23: wĕnātan mĕṭar zarʿekā ʾăšer tizraʿ ʾet-hāʾădāmâ = "and he will give rain for the seed (zereʿ) with which you sow the ground" — the covenant blessings of Deuteronomy restored: rain, abundant harvest, fat pastureland. wĕlāḥem tĕḇûʾat hāʾădāmâ wĕhāyâ dāšēn wĕšāmēn = "and the bread of the produce of the ground will be rich and plentiful (šāmēn)" — the land gives back to Israel what it withheld in judgment (cf. 5:10: the vineyard yielding almost nothing).
24: wĕhāʾĕlāpîm wĕhaʿăyārîm ʿōbĕdê hāʾădāmâ = "and the oxen and donkeys that work the soil" — working animals fed well on the threshed feed of restoration. bĕlîl ḥāmîṣ yōʾkēlû = "will eat salted/seasoned feed (bĕlîl ḥāmîṣ)" — the best feed for animals. Restoration extends to the humblest element of the agricultural economy: the well-fed working animal is the sign of a well-functioning covenant land.
25: wĕhāyâ ʿal-kol-har-gāḇohah ûʿal-kol-gibʿâ nĕśuʾâ pĕlāgîm yĕʿārôt mayim = "and on every high mountain and every exalted hill there will be streams of water." The inversion of the wilderness curse: bare heights that had no water (cf. 41:18) will flow with streams. The image is of a thoroughly watered, abundantly productive landscape — a reversal of the drought-judgment. bĕyôm hereḡ rāḇ bĕnepol migdālôt = "on the day of great slaughter, when towers fall" — this abundance follows a great military judgment; the restoration comes through and after the decisive defeat of the enemy.
26: wĕhāyâ ʾôr hallĕḇānâ kĕʾôr haḥammâ = "and the light (ʾôr) of the moon (lĕḇānâ) will be like the light of the sun (ḥammâ)" — the eschatological intensification of creation's light: lunar light (secondary) equals what was solar (primary). wĕʾôr haḥammâ yihyeh šiḇʿātayim kĕʾôr šiḇʿat hayyāmîm = "and the light of the sun will be sevenfold, like the light of seven days." The new creation's luminosity exceeds all prior calculation — light multiplied beyond its present capacity. Cf. Rev 21:23-25 where the new Jerusalem needs no sun or moon because God's glory illuminates it.
27: hinnēh šēm YHWH bāʾ mimmerhāq = "Behold, the Name (šēm) of YHWH comes from afar" — a theophany in which YHWH's character and presence (his šēm = Name = all that he is) approaches from a distance. bōʿēr ʾappô wĕkaḇĕd maśśāʾâ = "his anger (ʾap) burning and heavy is the burden (maśśāʾ)" — the theophany is not peaceful but judicial. śĕpātāyw māleʾû zaʿam wĕlĕšônô kĕʾēš ʾōkelet = "his lips are full of indignation (zaʿam) and his tongue is like a consuming fire (ʾēš ʾōkelet)" — the divine word as fire, consuming the enemy.
28: wĕrûḥô kĕnaḥal šôṭēp ʿad-ṣawwāʾr yaḥeṣeh = "and his breath (rûaḥ) is like an overflowing stream (naḥal šôṭēp) that reaches up to the neck" — the same flood imagery as 8:8 (the Assyrian reaching Judah's neck), now applied to YHWH's own breath/Spirit against Assyria. lāhānîp gôyim bĕnapâ šāwĕʾ = "to sift the nations with the sieve (nāpâ) of emptiness (šāwĕʾ)" — YHWH's breath as the refiner's sieve. wĕresen maṭʿeh ʿal lĕḥêy ʿammîm = "and a bridle that leads astray upon the jaws (lĕḥî) of the peoples" — YHWH bridles the nations as a rider bridles a horse, directing them to destruction.
29: haššîr yihyeh lākem kĕlêl hithaqqdĕšû-ḥāg = "The song will be for you as in the night when a feast is hallowed (hitqaddaš)" — the worship of the night before a sacred festival, probably Passover. wĕśimḥat lēḇāḇ kĕhôlēk bĕḥālîl = "and gladness of heart like one who marches with a flute (ḥālîl)" — the processional music of pilgrimage. lāḇôʾ bĕhar YHWH ʾel-ṣûr yiśrāʾēl = "to go to the mountain of YHWH, to the Rock (ṣûr) of Israel." In the midst of the judgment-oracle, this verse inserts a liturgical note of joy: the divine judgment of Assyria will be celebrated with the same joy as a Passover night.
30: wĕhišmîaʿ YHWH ʾet-qôl hadāratô = "and YHWH will cause the majestic voice (qôl hadāratô) to be heard" — the theophanic voice that judges. wĕnāḥat zĕrôʿô yirʾeh bĕzaʿam ʾap wĕlahab ʾēš ʾōkēlâ = "and the descent of his arm will be seen with furious anger (zaʿam), with devouring flame (lahab ʾēš ʾōkēlâ)" — the divine arm descending = direct intervention in battle. nepheṣ wāzerem ûʾeḇen bārād = "with cloudburst (zerem) and rainstorm and stones of hail" — the full arsenal of theophanic storm-weapons (cf. Josh 10:11 where YHWH fights with hailstones).
31: kî miqPĕnê YHWH yêḥat ʾAššûr = "For Assyria will be terrified (yêḥat, niphal of ḥātat) before the voice of YHWH" — the world empire that reduced nations to nothing is itself shattered by YHWH's voice alone. baššēḇeṭ yakkeh = "with a rod (šēḇeṭ) he will strike" — cf. 10:5 where Assyria was itself called YHWH's rod (šēḇeṭ); now YHWH's rod strikes the rod. The instrument of judgment is itself judged.
32: wĕhāyâ kol-maʿăḇar matteh hāmûsādâ = "and every stroke of the appointed staff" — mûsādâ = the staff of the appointed judgment, the Assyrian rod now redirected. wĕheyniîaḥ YHWH ʿālāyw bĕtupîm ûḇĕkinnōrôt = "YHWH will cause it to fall on them with tambourines and lyres" — the defeat of Assyria will be accompanied by worship music. ûḇĕmilḥamôt tĕnûpâ nilḥam bāh = "and in battles of shaking he will fight against them" — YHWH personally fighting against Assyria.
33: kî-ʿārûk mēʾetemôl Topteh = "For Topheth has long been prepared" — Tōpet = the altar of Molech in the Valley of Hinnom (Gehenna), where children were burned as offerings (Jer 7:31-32). Here Isaiah uses Topheth as a metaphor for the burning judgment prepared for the king of Assyria. gam-hûʾ lammelek hûkān = "even for the king it has been prepared." heʿĕmîq hirḥîḇ mĕdûrātāh ʾēš wĕʿēṣîm harbenû = "he has made the pyre deep and wide, with fire and wood in abundance" — the funeral pyre is outsized: for the most powerful king on earth, YHWH prepares a correspondingly massive burning. nišmat YHWH kĕnaḥal goprit bōʿărâ bāh = "and the breath (nišmat) of YHWH kindles it like a stream of burning sulfur (goprit)" — God's breath (the same word as Gen 2:7 where YHWH breathes life into Adam) now breathes death into Assyria's king. Topheth/Gehenna as eternal judgment appears in NT as the term Jesus uses for final destruction (Matt 5:22; Mark 9:43-48).
